Source_Species.reason: The paper states the enzymes screened were family 1 GTs from Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The Results explicitly say '85 family 1 GTs from Arabidopsis thaliana'.

Applied_Species.reason: UGT73C5 was expressed heterologously in E. coli lysate and also as purified enzyme for in vitro assays.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied host species is explicitly E. coli / Escherichia coli.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify Rosetta(DE3) pLysS for lysate screening and BL21*(DE3)-T1R for GST-tagged expression/purification.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study directly assays formation of glycosylated natural products/glucosides across many substrates using UDP-glucose.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Products are glycosides of diverse specialized/natural products throughout the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper generally states glycosylation in planta serves development, defense, communication and notes therapeutic relevance of many glycosides, but this is background-level and not directly demonstrated specifically for UGT73C5 products.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: UGT73C5 is one of the Arabidopsis family 1 glycosyltransferases; family 1 GTs are described as GT-B fold enzymes with PSPG motif, and Fig. 3c references the PSPG motif for UGT73C5.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Methods and Results show heterologous expression in E. coli for both lysate-based screening and purified GST-fusion assays.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly describe UGT73C5 as the most promiscuous enzyme, glycosylating up to 127 substrates in UDP-glucose-dependent assays.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are stated in Results: 127 substrates from 29 superclasses, 25 validated from 17 superclasses, and a roughly 1600 Å^3 pocket proposed to underlie promiscuity.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all explicitly used: lysate multiplex LC-MS/MS screen, purified GST-fusion assays, AlphaFold structural model inspection, and cavity analysis.

Applied_Promoters.reason: pET28a and pGEX-6P-1 expression vectors with IPTG induction are reported, but the paper does not explicitly name the promoter as T7 in the text provided.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify UDP-glucose, lysate assays at pH 6.8 and 30°C overnight, and purified assays at pH 7.6 and 25°C.

Omics_Data.reason: Mass spectrometry dataset is central to the paper, and raw files are stated to be at Zenodo record 15670423.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly states the results do not speak to enzyme functions in vivo and emphasize biochemical potential/capacity.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion explicitly states regioselectivity was not determined, in vivo function was not established, and assay outcomes depend on expression/conditions.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Results and Discussion say UGT73C5 may provide a unique scaffold for enzyme engineering and note implications for biocatalysis and metabolic engineering.

Source_Species.reason: UGT76C2 is among the Arabidopsis family 1 GTs screened.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The enzyme set is from Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: UGT76C2 was tested via heterologous expression in E. coli lysate and purified enzyme in vitro assays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Rosetta(DE3) pLysS was used for lysate screens and BL21*(DE3)-T1R for purified GST-tagged expression.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results explicitly show N-glycosylation products of 6-benzylaminopurine and 6-dimethylallylaminopurine and O-glycosylation products with other substrates; it is also stated UGT76C2 is responsible for N-glycosylation of cytokinins in Arabidopsis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The products are glycosides, including cytokinin/hormone conjugate-type N-glucosides and other glycosylated specialized metabolites.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: General functions of glycosylation are given in the introduction, and prior literature on cytokinin N-glycosylation is cited, but this paper does not directly test physiological effects on hormone metabolism.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results explicitly identify UGT76C2 as a family 1 GT with non-canonical Cys-Asp catalytic dyad.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper directly uses heterologous E. coli expression for lysate and purified assays.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results conclude UGT76C2 is bifunctional for O-/N-glycosylation and that WT favors N-glycosylation over O-glycosylation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each claim is in Results/Fig. 4: retained O-glycosylation despite Cys, stronger N-glycosylation on 6-benzylaminopurine and 6-dimethylallylaminopurine, and C20H/C20A/D112A mutation effects on O vs N glycosylation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described for UGT76C2: lysate LC-MS/MS, purified enzyme assays, mutagenesis, semi-quantitative LC-MS, structural modeling, and docking.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Vectors and IPTG induction are described, but the text provided does not explicitly state the promoter identity as T7.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Purified in vitro assays with UDP-glucose at 25°C and comparisons of WT and active-site mutants on O- and N-glycosylation substrates are explicitly described.

Omics_Data.reason: MS dataset and Zenodo record 15670423 are explicitly provided.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state UGT76C2 has previously been shown responsible for N-glycosylation of cytokinins in Arabidopsis, and the paper discusses possible non-canonical mechanisms.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper notes the precise chemoselectivity mechanism requires further investigation and does not establish full in planta substrate spectrum here.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Selective N-glycosylation potential is inferable from the findings, but explicit engineering application specifically for UGT76C2 is not directly stated beyond general implications for biocatalysis/metabolic engineering.

Source_Species.reason: UGT76C3 is one of the Arabidopsis family 1 GTs in the study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The screened enzymes are from Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: UGT76C3 was tested in E. coli lysate and purified in vitro assays.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify Escherichia coli expression hosts.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Rosetta(DE3) pLysS and BL21*(DE3)-T1R are explicitly listed in Methods for the two assay formats.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results explicitly show N-glycosylation products with 6-benzylaminopurine and 6-dimethylallylaminopurine and O-glycosylation activity on formononetin; therefore O-/N-glycosylated products are supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are glycosides, including purine/cytokinin-like conjugates and other glycosylated specialized metabolites.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: General glycosylation functions are provided for plant GTs overall, but no specific physiological function is established for UGT76C3 products in this paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: UGT76C3 is explicitly described as a family 1 GT with non-canonical Cys-Asp catalytic dyad.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Heterologous expression in E. coli for lysate and purified assays is directly described.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support bifunctional O-/N-glycosylation and a stronger N-glycosylation preference for the WT enzyme than the histidine mutant.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are directly supported in Results and Supplementary Fig. 23: retained O-glycosylation, strong N-glycosylation products, and shifts caused by C19H/C19A/D117A mutations.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper explicitly uses lysate screening, purified assays, mutagenesis, semi-quantitative LC-MS, and structural modeling for UGT76C3.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The vectors and IPTG induction are reported, but explicit promoter identity as T7 is not stated in the text provided.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: In vitro UDP-glucose reactions at 25°C with WT versus catalytic mutants are explicitly described.

Omics_Data.reason: The mass spectrometry dataset and Zenodo 15670423 are explicitly mentioned.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results discuss UGT76C3 together with UGT76C2 as evidence for non-canonical Cys-Asp-mediated N-glycosylation selectivity in plant family 1 GTs.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: It is fair and supported that native physiological roles/metabolite consequences are not directly established; Discussion emphasizes lack of in vivo functional conclusions.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential as an N-glycosylation biocatalyst is plausible, but the paper does not explicitly assign this specific application to UGT76C3 beyond broad implications for biocatalysis and metabolic engineering.
